title: elymus caninus subsp. donianus
intro: Elymus caninus subsp. donianus, also known as Don's wheat grass, is a subspecies of a perennial grass native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its slender stems and feathery panicles. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. Don's wheat grass is rich in nutrients and has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Elymus caninus subsp. donianus is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
